operating_state: Total revenue increased to $25.96 billion driven by 77% higher oil prices averaging $66.14 per barrel and 139% higher NGL prices, with production at 1,167 Mboe/d exceeding guidance. OxyChem achieved highest earnings in 30 years from record global PVC and caustic soda pricing. Hurricane Ida reduced output by approximately 2.5 million barrels through platform shutdowns, while Permian Basin operations set efficiency records.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased due to higher maintenance costs in aging Gulf of Mexico platforms and elevated energy costs in Permian, partially offset by operational efficiencies. Environmental operating expenses rose from higher oil and gas segment costs. Federal Superfund excise taxes on chemicals reinstated effective November 2021, while potential federal royalty rate increases and new seismic response guidelines may elevate future operating costs.
material_events: Completed $6.7 billion debt reduction through cash tenders and repayments, reducing face value from $35.2 billion to $28.5 billion. Entered amended credit agreement reducing revolving facility to $4.0 billion with sustainability features and SOFR benchmark extending maturity to June 2025. Board authorized new $3 billion share repurchase program while completing large-scale asset divestiture program including Ghana assets sale.
macro_exposures: Operations remain highly dependent on oil price volatility with Midland-to-Gulf-Coast spreads impacting annual operating cash flows by approximately $65 million per $0.25 change. Chemical segment margins face pressure from supply-demand imbalances, feedstock costs, supply chain interruptions and inflation. New Colorado regulations impose 500-foot setbacks though company received first permit approval under new COGCC regulations.
forward_outlook: Management established net-zero emissions goal for operations before 2040 requiring substantial capital investment in carbon management technologies. Capital budget set at $3.9-4.3 billion with approximately half allocated to Permian Basin development targeting sustained production while generating free cash flow. Five-year development plan commits approximately $3.0 billion to Permian Basin reserves with sub-two-year payback cycles.
uncertainty_profile: Recorded $1 billion uncertain tax position plus accrued interest for potential Anadarko Tronox tax dispute with U.S. Tax Court trial scheduled July 2022. Zero Coupon notes totaling $1.1 billion can be put to company in October 2022 requiring potential refinancing. Environmental remediation liabilities exceed $1.1 billion across multiple sites with potential additional losses up to $1.3 billion.
